name: DOXI Sign API
description:
# Getting Started Welcome to the **Doxi Sign API**! This guide explains how to authenticate and use key endpoints like creating flows and using templates. Swagger: [https://doxi-sign.com/api/swagger](https://doxi-sign.com/api/swagger) OpenAPI: [https://doxi-sign.com/api/swagger/v4/swagger.json](https://doxi-sign.com/api/swagger/v4/swagger.json) --- ## Get Access Token (Login) All API requests require a valid access token. ### Token Endpoint Format POST [https://login.doxi-sign.com/auth/realms/{tenant}/protocol/openid-connect/token](https://login.doxi-sign.com/realms/%7Btenant%7D/protocol/openid-connect/token) > Replace `{tenant}` with your actual company name. For example, if your Doxi URL is `https://doxi-sign.com/mycompany`, then: POST [https://login.doxi-sign.com/auth/realms/mycompany/protocol/openid-connect/token](https://login.doxi-sign.com/realms/mycompany/protocol/openid-connect/token) ### Headers ### Body Parameters | Field | Value | | --- | --- | | grant_type | password | | client_id | doxi | | username | [your@email.com](https://mailto:your@email.com) | | password | your_password | | scope | openid | > Only `username`, `password`, and `{tenant}` need to be changed. `client_id` is always `doxi`. ### Sample Response ``` json { "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6Ikp...", "expires_in": 300, "refresh_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6Ikp...", "token_type": "Bearer" } ``` ## Required Headers for All API Calls Every request to the Doxi API must include the following headers: | Header | Description | | --- | --- | | Authorization | `Bearer {access_token}` – the token obtained from the login step | | X-Tenant | Your tenant name (e.g., `mycompany`) | ### Example: Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6Ikp... X-Tenant: mycompany > Your tenant name appears in your Doxi URL: If your portal is `https://doxi-sign.com/mycompany`, your `X-Tenant` is `mycompany`. ## Refresh Token When your access token expires, you can use the `refresh_token` to obtain a new access token without logging in again. ### Endpoint POST [https://login.doxi-sign.com/realms/{tenant}/protocol/openid-connect/token](https://login.doxi-sign.com/realms/%7Btenant%7D/protocol/openid-connect/token) > Replace `{tenant}` with your actual tenant name (e.g., `mycompany`). ### Headers ### Body Parameters | Field | Value | | --- | --- | | grant_type | refresh_token | | client_id | doxi | | refresh_token | your_refresh_token | ### Sample Request POST [https://login.doxi-sign.com/realms/mycompany/protocol/openid-connect/token](https://login.doxi-sign.com/realms/mycompany/protocol/openid-connect/token) Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded grant_type=refresh_token& client_id=doxi& refresh_token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6Ikp... ``` json { "access_token": "new-access-token", "refresh_token": "new-refresh-token", "expires_in": 300, "token_type": "Bearer" } ``` Make sure to update the Authorization header in future API calls with the new access_token ## Create a New Flow This method creates a signature flow in the Doxi system by uploading a PDF document and defining the process using a JSON payload. --- ### Endpoint POST [https://doxi-sign.com/api/v6/flow](https://doxi-sign.com/api/ex/flow) --- ### Required Headers X-Tenant: mycompany Content-Type: --- ### Body Parameters (multipart/form-data) | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `File` | File (PDF) | The PDF document to be signed, uploaded as binary stream | | `CreateFlowJsonRequest` | String (JSON) | The full flow configuration in JSON format | --- ### Example Request **Form Data Fields:** - `File`: Upload the actual PDF file (`application/pdf`) - `CreateFlowJsonRequest`: Paste this JSON string: ``` json { "DocumentFileName": "API Example.pdf", "SenderKey": { "Key": "someUser@SomeDomain.com", "Type": 1 }, "Description": "API Example test", "FlowElements": [ { "SignerKey": { "Key": "someUser@SomeDomain.com", "Type": 1 }, "ElementType": 0, "ElementSubType": 0, "PageNumber": 1, "Position": { "Top": 440.2, "Left": 62.0, "Width": 78.8, "Height": 37.5 } } ], "Recipients": [ { "UserKey": { "Key": "david@doxi.com", "Type": 1 }, "Email": "david@doxi.com", "FirstName": "David", "LastName": "White" } ], "CustomFields": [ { "Key": "SomeInnerSystemIdNumber", "Value": "12345" } ], "Users": [ { "UserKey": { "Key": "someUser@SomeDomain.com", "Type": 1 }, "Email": "someUser@SomeDomain.com", "FirstName": "Marty", "LastName": "Braun", "SmsPhoneNumber": "0501234567", "ForceApprovalOnSignature": false, "PersonalMessage": "test 123" } ] } ``` The CreateFlowJsonRequest must be a string field containing valid JSON, not a nested object. ### 🧠 Field Explanations | Field | Description | | --- | --- | | `DocumentFileName` | The display name of the PDF document that will appear in the signing process. | | `SenderKey` | Identifies the user who initiated the signing process. This user receives the final signed document. It includes:
• `Key`: usually the sender's email
• `Type`: key type (1 = Email) | | `Description` | A short message that describes the purpose of the signing flow. Shown to signers in emails and signing screens. | | `FlowElements` | An array of field definitions (elements) that need to be filled during the signing process. Each element includes:
• `SignerKey`: who must fill the element
• `ElementType`: type of field (e.g., signature, checkbox, text)
• `ElementSubType`: further categorization (optional)
• `PageNumber`: which page of the document
• `Position`: coordinates and size (Top, Left, Width, Height) | | `Recipients` | A list of users who will receive email notifications when the flow is signed or rejected. These users are not signers. | | `CustomFields` | Optional metadata fields (key-value pairs) attached to the flow. Useful for connecting the flow to internal system IDs or business context. | | `Users` | List of signers involved in the flow. Each user must have at least an email or phone number.
Additional properties include:
• `SmsPhoneNumber`: if SMS is used
• `ForceApprovalOnSignature`: forces confirmation after signature
• `PersonalMessage`: custom message sent to the signer | ### Pro Tip: Reverse-Engineer Flow Configuration To determine the exact **element positions**, **field types**, and all required parameters for creating a signing flow via the API, it's recommended to first build a sample process using the **Doxi web interface**. Once the flow is created manually, you can retrieve its full configuration using the following API: GET /ex/flow/{signFlowId} This will return all relevant details (including users, elements, field positions, and structure) which you can then reuse to construct the same flow programmatically via the API.
